feed_content: Welcome to pglite-feed — a browser-only microblog powered by PGlite (Postgres in WebAssembly).
All data lives in your browser's IndexedDB. Nothing is sent to a server.
Use the ✚ button to create entries. Click ✎ to edit or ✕ to delete. Ctrl+Enter or Shift+Enter submits, Esc cancels.
Use the ↓ button to export a JSON backup, ↑ to load one. Great for transferring between browsers, restoring, or sharing content.
Organize with #hashtags in your content. Tagged entries are hidden from the default view — type # in the search bar to browse all tags as a cloud.
Search supports multi-word AND matching, -exclusion, after: and before: date filtering, and [linkable searches](?search=%23demo) via URL parameters.
Use [markdown links](https://example.com) or paste bare URLs like https://example.com — both become clickable. External links open in a new tab.
Customize the site title with !title My Site and the color theme with !theme amber, !theme white, or !theme green (default). Settings persist and are included in JSON exports.
Read more [about this app](assets/about.md) — rendered inline with the retro theme, complete with table of contents.
Fork this repo and deploy to your own domain — [pglite-feed on GitHub](https://github.com/robertvigil/pglite-feed)
These are sample entries that loaded on first visit. Edit or delete them, or use ↑ to load your own JSON. #demo
Tagged entries like this one are hidden from the default view. Search for #demo or type # to find them. #demo
